eval_protocol.activation_test: 10 prompts that should activate the skill; target ≥9/10 activation
eval_protocol.rejection_test: 5 prompts that should NOT activate the skill; target 0/5 activation
eval_protocol.output_quality_test: 1 representative prompt with expected output criteria

evals.prompt: I found this API called PubChem that has supplement compound data. I want to build a pipeline that pulls all the data for the top 50 supplements (vitamin D, magnesium, zinc, etc.) every night at 2am and stores it in my PostgreSQL database on the proxmox server. Can you help me set up the schema and the pipeline script?
evals.expected_output: Complete PostgreSQL CREATE TABLE statements with provenance tracking (source_id, source_name, ingested_at), a full Python pipeline script following the extract-validate-transform-load-verify-log pattern with error handling and retry logic, and scheduling configuration (cron or Prefect/Dagster)
activation_phrases: build a pipeline for health data
how do I store lab results
rejection_phrases: build a web scraper for news

evals.prompt: I've got my biohacking database and python pipelines running on my proxmox box and it's working well locally. I want to start moving this to GCP but I'm nervous about the cost and about breaking things. Where do I even start? I don't want to spend more than $30/month to start.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step migration plan starting with containerization, GCP service recommendations (Cloud SQL, Cloud Run), cost estimate showing it fits within $30/month, and risk mitigation strategies including parallel running
activation_phrases: how do I get this running for real users

evals.prompt: I keep hearing about personalized health and longevity being huge markets. I want to understand if building this biohacking AI tool is actually a good business or if I should be doing something else. Can you do a deep analysis of the opportunity and suggest alternative businesses I could build?
evals.expected_output: TAM/SAM/SOM analysis of personalized health market, competitive landscape mapping with named competitors, profit mechanics analysis, 5-10 alternative business ideas scored against the opportunity framework, clear recommendation with reasoning
activation_phrases: is this a good business to build
where is the money in AI
rejection_phrases: how do I do keyword research

evals.prompt: I need to get my website up. I do AI consulting AND I'm building a biohacking product. Should these be one website or two? What should the site look like? I want it to look professional and cutting-edge but not corporate.
evals.expected_output: One-brand-vs-two analysis with recommendation, complete visual identity (hex codes, font pairings, logo direction), full site architecture with page-by-page content outline, tech stack recommendation, and SEO strategy
activation_phrases: what colors should my site use
should I have one site or two
rejection_phrases: how do I set up Next.js
how do I rank on Google

evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Python project for my biohacking API — FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Docker, deploying to Cloud Run. I use Claude Code, Cursor, and sometimes Codex. Can you generate all the project instruction files I need?
evals.expected_output: Complete, ready-to-use CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, .cursorrules, and .github/copilot-instructions.md files, all consistent with each other but tailored to each tool's strengths
activation_phrases: create project instructions for Claude Code
how do I configure Codex for my project
make AI tools work better with my codebase
AI tool setup for new project
rejection_phrases: how do I use Claude Code

evals.prompt: I need a new skill for working with the Anthropic Claude API — specifically for building production apps that use tool use, streaming responses, and prompt caching. I want a SKILL.md file that can be used with Claude Code. Can you build it using the skill-builder methodology?
evals.expected_output: A complete SKILL.md following the 9-section canonical template, with: engineered description field, metadata block with all required fields, anti-patterns section with ≥3 named anti-patterns, failure modes with detection signals and fallbacks, composability section naming adjacent skills, quality gates, and SKILL.md body ≤500 lines.
activation_phrases: write a skill for this domain
build skills for the engineering category
rejection_phrases: how do I use slash commands

evals.expected_output: Structured findings by severity: CRITICAL for SQL injection via f-string, HIGH for bare except swallowing all errors and returning a vague error response, HIGH for missing return type hint and sync database call in what should be an async endpoint, MEDIUM for SELECT * instead of specific columns, with specific corrected code for each finding and a summary verdict of REQUEST CHANGES.
rejection_phrases: how do I make this faster

evals.prompt: I have a FastAPI endpoint that reads supplement data from PostgreSQL and returns interaction warnings. How should I test it? I'm using pytest. I want to make sure I'm testing it correctly and not just mocking everything.
evals.expected_output: Specific pytest strategy: use AsyncClient with ASGITransport for endpoint tests, use a real test database with the transaction-rollback fixture pattern (not mocks), at least one integration test against real DB, parametrize for multiple supplement combinations, hypothesis strategy for edge cases, and explicit guidance on NOT mocking SQLAlchemy.
activation_phrases: how do I test async code
unit test vs integration test for this
mock vs real database in tests

evals.prompt: I need to design a schema for storing supplement interaction data. Each supplement can interact with many other supplements (the interaction is bidirectional), and each interaction has a severity (low/medium/high), a mechanism, and links to research papers. I also need to track where the data came from and when it was ingested. What's the right schema?
evals.expected_output: Normalized schema DDL with: supplements table, supplement_interactions junction table (handling bidirectionality via ordered pair or explicit both-directions rows with justification), research_papers table, interaction_evidence join table, provenance columns (source_id, source_name, ingested_at) on external-data tables, indexes on all foreign keys, a CHECK constraint on severity enum, and COMMENT ON TABLE/COLUMN documentation for non-obvious design decisions.
activation_phrases: how should I model this in postgres
how do I store temporal data in postgres
rejection_phrases: write a migration for this schema
how do I set up Cloud SQL
how do I connect to postgres
write tests for my database code
